disambiguated.context: I joined a new support group recently for people trying dieting, and I met two people my first day who were really nice to me. One was overweight and the other was really thin. The next week that I went to the dieting support group, though, I only saw the overweight person, and was later told that the really thin one had decided to leave the group since they didn't want to diet anymore.
disambiguated.question: Who gave up on their diet?

stages.reason: The context states the 'really thin one had decided to leave the group since they didn't want to diet anymore.' This directly indicates they gave up on dieting.
The context states the thin person left because they didn't want to diet anymore, directly answering who gave up on their diet. The overweight person continued attending.
The text explicitly states the really thin person left the group because they no longer wanted to diet, indicating they gave up on it.
